---
title: Migrating from R blogdown to Quarto blogging
author: Jeffrey S. Racine
date: 20123-08-11
draft: false
---

Ahh, the joys and sorrows of technological progress! 

Some background is likely in order (and an apology for neglecting my blog). In 2018 I discovered R blogdown which was a big advance (for me at least) in creating a blog. The use of RStudio/blogdown for building websites was a very straightforward matter, and some features that streamlined creating new posts are in fact currently missing from my newly migrated quarto blogging framework.^[at the moment, at least - the latter is an evolution of the former by the same team of developers, so one might expect this to re-appear.] However, the R blogdown framework came with a set of issues, and having to actively maintain a theme and modify the theme to accomplish certain tasks created conditions for that blog incarnation's eventual neglect (the *hugo* theme for the curious). In addition, I was using the free hosting platform netlify for hosting the site, and leveraging its ability to pull updated code from a GitHub repo and automatically rebuild the blog. But, after not too much time had elapsed since adopting this approach, this framework became unmaintainable (read "not worth the effort" - netlify issuing security warnings about the hugo theme, issues with GitHub's security features and moving away from passwords, etc., all conspired against this adopted framework). In short, I was unwilling to invest the time to debug and keep this framework current, hence it lapsed which explains the large gap between some posts (and my offer of an apology).

But with my recent experiences using Quarto being quite positive, and with Quarto^[Quarto is a multi-language, next-generation version of R Markdown from Posit and includes dozens of new features and capabilities while at the same being able to render most existing Rmd files without modification.] broadening its embrace beyond RStudio and R (and re-branding as Posit), it is clear that clinging to R blogdown was a recipe for pain that I didn't need.

So, while visiting a co-author at the University of Pretoria this month, and after the jet lag had passed, I decided to dedicate part of this morning to migrating my old R blogdown code to the Quarto blogging framework and to host it directly on GitHub Pages (I am a big fan of GitHub Pages by the way).

The migration was very straightforward and in less than one hour I was up and running with the migrated site (probably closer to 1/2 an hour of work was required, at most). So far I am very happy with the result, and there is far less to maintain than when using R blogdown (at least this is my recollection). And hopefully this platform turns out to be more "future-proofed" than its predecessor. I am more than happy to discuss details with you if you wish (shoot me an email!). 

Time will tell if this newly adopted framework is more resilient than its predecessor.
